jobs.Data Scientist: As a member of our Consulting team, you will build long-term relationships and collaborate closely with global clients to solve their most complex challenges. You’ll work alongside industry visionaries to accelerate digital transformation and AI integration for innovative organizations worldwide. Your ability to drive meaningful change will be supported by a diverse partner ecosystem and a robust suite of technology platforms. Role and Responsibilities Model Development: Design and develop AI and Generative AI (GenAI) models tailored for Healthcare and Life Sciences applications. Data Analysis: Analyze large structured and unstructured datasets to extract actionable insights. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with clinicians, biostatisticians, and software engineers to integrate AI solutions into real-world clinical workflows. Predictive Analytics: Develop and validate models for patient outcomes, treatment optimization, and clinical decision support. Advanced AI Implementation: Build agentic AI systems capable of autonomous task execution or providing intelligent recommendations within healthcare processes. Evaluation & Quality: Conduct experiments and performance evaluations to ensure model accuracy, fairness, and reliability. Innovation: Stay current with AI/ML trends, particularly in GenAI, to propose and implement cutting-edge solutions. Documentation: Maintain detailed records of methodologies, results, and pipelines for reproducibility and regulatory compliance. Deployment: Support the deployment and continuous monitoring of AI solutions in production environments. Required Technical and Professional Expertise AI/ML Mastery: Proven experience in AI/GenAI and machine learning development, specifically with deep learning and agentic systems. Programming: Strong proficiency in Python, R, or similar languages, and hands-on experience with frameworks like TensorFlow, PyTorch, or scikit-learn. Domain Knowledge: Experience working with healthcare or life sciences data (e.g., EHR, genomic, clinical trial, or biomedical datasets). Data Science Fundamentals: Solid understanding of statistics, experimental design, feature engineering, and model validation. Cloud & Deployment: Familiarity with major cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and the operationalization of AI models. Communication: Ability to translate complex technical findings into insights for both technical teams and non-technical stakeholders (e.g., clinicians or product managers).
jobs.Software Engineer:
The Opportunity

Are you inspired by digital transformation and impacting the lives of millions of users every day? We are looking for an empathetic, curious, and persistent Software Engineer to join our dynamic, inclusive team. In this role, you won't just write code; you will design what’s next. To thrive here, you must be enthusiastic about taking on stretch assignments and eager to incorporate emerging technologies into high-quality results.

What You’ll Do

Build Thriving Teams: As an experienced technical leader, you will share knowledge, motivate your peers, and keep the team engaged and productive.

Create Polished Results: Take a proactive, hands-on approach to development. You never leave a task unfinished and ensure every product delivered is as polished as possible.

Drive Continuous Learning: Actively collaborate with other associates to share ideas, stay ahead of changing technology trends, and build superior tools for our clients.

Influence & Inspire: Confidently present milestones and technical roadmaps to senior leadership and product owners with a clear, compelling voice.

Solve Complex Challenges: Work within an Agile/Scrum framework to design, build, and integrate new software components while evolving cloud-based enterprise products.

Architectural Oversight: Define standards and patterns to ensure quality and velocity. Participate in API-driven microservices architecture and automated testing.

Requirements for Success

Education:
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field plus five (5) years of experience, OR a Master’s degree plus two (2) years of related experience.

Technical Experience:

SaaS & Architecture: Proven experience with transactional, distributed SaaS applications and Distributed Microservice Architecture.

Backend Development: Mastery of Java and server-side frameworks (e.g., Spring, Spring Boot, Servlets, JSP).

Data & Persistence: Expertise in ORMs (Hibernate), RDBMS (MySQL or similar), and database query optimization.

Modern Engineering: Experience with Test-Driven Development (TDD), Functional Programming, and Restful web services.

Security & Quality: Deep understanding of Secure Coding Practices and building/supporting shared software libraries.

Why You’ll Love Working Here

Courageous Collaboration: We encourage associates to be owners, speak up, and challenge ideas to find the best solutions.

Epic Scale: We focus on achieving simplicity at scale, obsessed with user productivity and "client happiness."

Growth Mindset: Surround yourself with curious learners in an environment that encourages listening, staying agile, and learning from mistakes.

Mission-Driven: No task is too small or too great. We are hands-on "doers" who are committed to mastering our craft.

Community Impact: We believe in doing the right thing for our clients and giving back to the communities where we live and work.
jobs.Investment Banker:
Business Overview

Our Investment Banking and Capital Markets division provides a comprehensive suite of financial advisory and capital-raising services to large-cap and mid-cap corporations and financial sponsors. We leverage a globally integrated platform to deliver world-class expertise in Mergers & Acquisitions (M&A), restructuring, and debt and equity capital markets. With a strong presence in innovative financial instruments and ESG-focused solutions, we enable clients to navigate complex, multi-dimensional cross-border and regional transactions.

What We Offer

Inclusive Culture: A diverse environment that embraces innovation, change, and collaboration.

Flexible Work Model: A hybrid working structure providing a balance between in-office collaboration and remote flexibility.

Comprehensive Benefits: Competitive compensation packages, including health and wellness benefits, retirement savings plans, and family-building support.

Professional Development: Access to global educational resources, employee resource groups, and community engagement programs.

Role & Responsibilities

Transaction Execution: Support the execution of live transactions for the sector coverage team, including the preparation of detailed memoranda, pitch books, and client presentations.

Financial Analysis: Apply rigorous financial, economic, and quantitative methods to evaluate and execute acquisitions, divestitures, and capital-raising activities.

Modeling: Develop and maintain advanced financial models (DCF, LBO, Accretion/Dilution, and Comparable Company Analysis) to support valuation and strategic decision-making.

Communication: Synthesize complex data into reports for senior management and maintain ongoing professional communication with client stakeholders.

Leadership & Team Impact

Executive Presence: Proven ability to work alongside senior management while maintaining the highest levels of confidentiality and discretion.

Initiative: Strong leadership skills with the capacity to take ownership of workstreams and assume responsibility for deal deliverables.

Collaboration: Foster a positive, effective work environment conducive to open communication and collective problem-solving.

Required Skills & Qualifications

Transaction Experience: Proven experience providing analytical support for M&A and capital markets transactions (IPOs, equity follow-ons, and debt offerings).

Sector Expertise: Prior background in sector-specific coverage (e.g., Consumer, Retail, or Business Services) or a dedicated M&A group.

Technical Proficiency: Mastery of detailed financial modeling and valuation analyses for both public and private entities.

Education: Bachelor’s degree required; an advanced degree (MBA/JD) or CFA designation is preferred.

Licensing: Relevant regulatory licenses (e.g., Series 79, 63, and SIE) are preferred or required shortly after hire.

Success Factors

The ideal candidate will excel by performing in-depth industry research to support live deal execution and proactive pitching. You should be comfortable navigating all stages of a transaction lifecycle—from initial "bake-offs" and marketing material preparation to final closing and execution.
jobs.Consultant:
Are you a driven, analytical professional with a passion for problem-solving and data-driven insights? We are seeking an Analyst Consultant to join our Risk Analytics practice. In this role, you will help global financial services clients navigate complex risk management challenges by developing innovative, quantitative solutions and strategic recommendations.

Working alongside a team of experienced advisors, you will leverage your expertise in risk modeling and advanced analytics to drive impactful results for organizations operating in high-stakes environments.

What You’ll Do

In-Depth Data Analysis: Conduct rigorous analysis of client data to provide actionable insights and improve overarching risk management strategies.

Solution Development: Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design innovative frameworks for risk assessment, mitigation, and monitoring.

Trend Identification: Utilize advanced analytical skills to identify emerging market trends, patterns, and potential risk vectors.

Strategic Communication: Synthesize complex data into clear, concise narratives and presentations for both technical teams and executive stakeholders.

Technical Modeling: Utilize specialized tools to develop data-driven models and simulations to forecast potential financial outcomes.

Project Leadership: Manage specific workstreams and deliverables to ensure timely, high-quality execution of client engagements.

Knowledge Leadership: Act as a subject matter expert in risk analytics, providing guidance to junior team members and staying current on industry best practices.

Required Technical Skills

Quantitative Analysis: Deep proficiency in statistical analysis, data modeling, and visualization software.

Risk Principles: Strong foundational knowledge of risk management techniques, including stress testing, credit risk, market risk, or operational risk.

Financial Modeling: Experience in financial analysis, forecasting, and strategic thinking.

Project Management: Ability to lead project tasks within an agile, fast-paced consulting environment.

Qualifications

Education: Strong academic background in a quantitative field (e.g., Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, Engineering, or Finance).

Experience: Prior experience in risk analytics, financial services, or management consulting is highly desirable.

Communication: Exceptional presentation skills with the ability to convey technical findings to non-technical audiences.

Soft Skills for Success

Leadership & Teamwork: Ability to lead through influence and collaborate effectively in a diverse team setting.

Emotional Intelligence: High EQ to navigate client relationships and internal team dynamics.

Adaptability: Flexibility to handle shifting priorities and complex, evolving problem sets.

Problem-Solving: A creative approach to troubleshooting and methodology improvement.
jobs.Actuarial Analyst:
We are seeking a data-driven Actuarial Analyst II to support forecasting, valuation, and pricing processes within our health insurance division. In this role, you will be instrumental in managing the actuarial models that drive our pricing strategies, trend analytics, and financial projections for Affordable Care Act (ACA) and related markets.

You will work at the intersection of traditional actuarial science and modern technology, ensuring our models are robust, automated, and strategically sound.

Responsibilities

Pricing & Modeling: Maintain and refine core pricing and plan design models. Ensure standardization and consistency across all users and markets.

Regulatory Filings: Serve as a key contributor to regulatory filing development, providing data-backed recommendations for strategic pricing decisions.

Reserving & Accruals: Develop and recommend accrual settings for Incurred but not Reported (IBNR) claims, Risk Adjustment, and value-based care/shared savings arrangements.

Experience Analysis: Identify and triage significant trends in medical experience across different plan designs, provider networks, and geographical markets.

Operational Excellence: Promote actuarial best practices by championing automation and maintaining high standards for documentation and reproducibility.

Cross-Functional Collaboration: Build strong lines of communication with internal stakeholders and assist in the onboarding and training of junior analysts.

Compliance: Ensure all work products comply with relevant state and federal laws and actuarial standards of practice.

Requirements

Education: Bachelor’s degree in a STEM field (e.g., Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or Economics).

Exams: Successful completion of at least two (2) actuarial exams (SOA/CAS).

Experience: 2+ years of professional experience in an actuarial or insurance analytics field.

Preferred Qualifications

Technical Proficiency: Strong coding skills (e.g., Python, SQL, or R) and a desire to apply tech-forward methodologies to traditional actuarial workflows.

Health Domain Knowledge: Understanding of health insurance nuances, including risk adjustment, medical loss ratios (MLR), and ACA regulations.

Analytical Mindset: Ability to translate complex data sets into actionable business insights.
jobs.Accountant:
Overview

Our Finance team is the backbone of our organizational security, overseeing everything from financial planning to risk and fraud analysis. We are seeking a diligent Accounts Payable Accountant to manage the timely processing of trade vendor invoices, employee expense reimbursements, and customer transactions.

In this role, you will be a guardian of internal controls, ensuring that all payments are properly authorized, documented, and executed with precision.

Responsibilities

Transaction Management: Process high-volume transactions, including multi-currency invoices and employee expense reports.

Audit & Compliance: Perform weekly audits of Travel & Expense (T&E) reports; provide policy guidance and resources to employees to ensure organizational alignment.

System Integrity: Configure and troubleshoot system workflows to ensure accurate account coding, proper authorization, and data integrity.

Three-Way Matching: Perform matching of Purchase Orders (PO), receivers, and invoices, collaborating with internal departments to resolve discrepancies.

Vendor Relations: Manage the reconciliation of vendor statements and proactively resolve internal and external inquiries regarding account status.

Month-End Close: Support the Accounting and FP&A teams during the month-end closing process, meeting strict deadlines and assisting in variance resolution.

Reporting: Generate and distribute monthly reports tracking expense activities and adherence to corporate financial policies.

Process Improvement: Identify opportunities to streamline the "Purchase-to-Pay" workstream, utilizing technology to automate disbursements and enhance efficiency.

Audit Readiness: Maintain accurate digital and physical files, providing necessary documentation and evidence for internal and external audits.

Requirements

Experience: Minimum of 3 years of dedicated experience in Accounts Payable or a general accounting function.

Education: Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or Business preferred.

Technical Skills: Solid understanding of AP financial systems and procedures.

Proficiency in MS Office (Excel/Word).

Familiarity with foreign currency transactions.

Analytical Skills: Strong research and multitasking abilities with a keen eye for detail.

Key Competencies

Integrity: Unquestionable ethics and the ability to handle confidential financial information with discretion.

Organization: Ability to prioritize a heavy workload to meet critical payment and closing deadlines.

Communication: Strong interpersonal skills to effectively resolve discrepancies across departments.

Initiative: A proactive teammate who seeks out ways to improve existing methodologies.
